briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 21, 2026. Today, we have a diverse range of news, from warnings highlighting the severity of the climate crisis to efforts both domestically and internationally to achieve carbon neutrality and expand renewable energy.

sections.title: ⚠️ Worsening Climate Crisis and Potential Risks
sections.content: Recently, heatwaves and heavy rainfall have become commonplace in Korea, leading to calls for urgent strengthening of inter-ministerial responses to the climate crisis [11]. Direct impacts of climate change are being observed in the agricultural sector, with frost damage to chili pepper crops and high-temperature phenomena in aquaculture environments [13, 15]. Furthermore, the World Heritage site Machu Picchu is facing a crisis due to climate change, with conservation cooperation underway [16, 31]. Warnings that the Earth's 'smell' is disappearing and culture is changing suggest that the climate crisis is deeply affecting our lives [23]. Notably, a warning that concealment and silence regarding the climate crisis could lead to severe consequences like a 'mega tsunami' [33], along with criticism that wood-fired power generation emits more carbon than gas, indicates the need for a thorough review of carbon neutrality policies [49]. Internationally, news of rising home insurance costs and difficulties in the World Bank's climate change action plan reminds us that the climate crisis poses a significant challenge across the economy and international cooperation [203, 211, 216].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Response Efforts and the Spread of Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Various local governments and institutions in Korea are actively engaging in climate change response and carbon neutrality practices. Jeollanam-do Province has proposed to the government to host the UN Climate Change Conference (COP) and is making multifaceted efforts to respond to the climate crisis, including constructing deep underground rainwater tunnels in Gangnam Station and Gwanghwamun, and improving the production system for forest tree seedlings [1, 6, 25, 29, 38]. Numerous local governments, including Yangpyeong-gun, Suyeong-gu, Jangseong-gun, Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province, and Incheon City, operated 'Climate Change Week,' encouraging public awareness and action through environmental book and climate photo exhibitions, lights-out events, and carbon neutrality campaigns [2, 3, 14, 17, 18, 21, 24, 28, 34, 35, 53, 59, 80, 85]. Furthermore, the launch of Pyeongtaek City's '2050 Carbon Neutral Green Growth Committee' [46, 50, 61, 62, 69, 81, 96, 100, 102, 103, 104] and the Local Government Carbon Neutrality Forum held in Yeosu [37, 39, 40, 41, 52, 58, 73, 95, 98, 99, 101, 107] have laid important groundwork for the spread of region-led carbon neutrality implementation. Specific projects such as Sinan-gun's selection for a pilot aquaculture support project for climate change response [4, 15, 22, 26, 30], Geoje City's full-scale promotion of a climate change experience tourism garden project [8, 9, 12], and KOSPO's (Korea Midland Power) carbon neutrality technology demonstration [10, 19, 32, 42, 83, 106] are also actively being pursued, strengthening Korea's climate change response capabilities.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Acceleration of Green Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is emerging as a core pillar of climate change response, with both domestic and international companies and governments expanding investments in this sector. Notably, Naver has directly invested equity in GS Wind Power Plant and signed a direct PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) for renewable energy, becoming the first RE100 (100% Renewable Energy Use) signatory in Korea to directly invest in a power generation company [110, 111, 112, 117, 119, 134, 135, 136, 137, 139, 145, 146, 150, 153, 157, 161, 165, 173, 177, 179, 182, 189, 192, 193, 196, 199, 201]. This is seen as an effort to strengthen the stability of renewable energy supply for data centers and lead eco-friendly management. GS E&C (Engineering & Construction) is accelerating its penetration into the Indian renewable energy market, expanding partnerships with local companies for wind repowering, solar power, and integrated power projects including ESS (Energy Storage Systems) [114, 118, 120, 122, 123, 125, 126, 128, 129, 130, 131, 138, 143, 144, 147, 149, 151, 155, 158, 160, 162, 163, 164, 168, 169, 170, 171, 172, 176, 178, 180, 183, 184, 185, 186, 187, 188, 190, 191, 195, 198, 200]. Domestically, with arguments that Korea's energy security depends on the expansion of renewable energy and that the share of renewable energy should be increased to over 20% by 2030 [121], discussions are also actively underway to explore power grid and renewable energy solutions to meet electricity demand in the AI era [115, 124, 194]. Furthermore, news that renewable energy is expected to surpass coal to become the world's largest power source by 2025 demonstrates the acceleration of the global green transition [217].

sections.title: 🌍 International Discussions and Search for Solutions to Climate Change
sections.content: In the international community, various discussions and searches for solutions to climate change issues are ongoing. Discussions on specific measures are taking place, such as the analysis suggesting that 'everything must be electrified' to solve climate change [202]. There are also active movements exploring the nature and impacts of climate change under themes like 'People and Climate Change 2026' [204, 205, 209]. Dartmouth Health is attempting to address climate impacts in the health sector by launching a program focused on the link between climate change and cancer treatment [206]. At the UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues, war, climate change, and AI were discussed as key agenda items, highlighting the multifaceted aspects of the climate crisis [207]. Meanwhile, the importance of thorough review and critical thinking regarding climate solutions is also being emphasized, with the publication of a book that critically views imported woody biomass power generation as a 'wrong climate change solution' [208]. News that U.S. military officials are taking action to address the climate crisis while avoiding the term 'climate crisis' shows that climate action is taking place even amidst political sensitivities [212], and the UNFCCC's 2026 Petersberg Climate Dialogue is expected to build international momentum towards COP31 [213].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted warnings about the severity of the climate crisis, alongside active efforts towards carbon neutrality and the accelerated transition to renewable energy, both domestically and internationally. We hope that these multifaceted approaches will continue and contribute to overcoming the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.
